title: ericameria parishii var. parishii
intro: Ericameria parishii var. parishii, also known as Parish's golden bush or Parish's greasewood, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the sunflower family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and resin. The health benefits of Ericameria parishii var. parishii include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, salves, and tinctures, to utilize its medicinal properties for various health issues.
